english: application, study, exertion, _bád-pa drág-pos ('bad pa drag pos) with most persevering application; _bád-pa dań rtsól-ba méd-par ('bad pa dang rtsol ba med par) without any exertion Glr.; hence _bad-rtsól ('bad rtsol) id.; skyés-bus srúb-pai _bad-rtsól (skyes bus srub pa'i 'bad rtsol) an assiduous rubbing with a human hand Wdn.; dei _bad-rtsól-gyis (de'i 'bad rtsol gyis) through his endeavours Thgy.; prob. also: volition, energy of will S.g.; the passage in Thgy.: byań-čúb či tób-la _bad tsál-lo (byang chub ci thob la 'bad tsal lo), is perh. not quite correct.
1. a fall of snow Mil. — 2. tax, duty Sp.

pf. bab(s) (bab(s)), imp. _bob ('bob) Cs., bobs (bobs) Glr., to move downward 1. to descend, lá-nas (la nas) col., a defile, in B. gen. with las (las), e.g. rtá-las (rta las) Dzl., also rtá-k`a-nas (rta kha nas) Glr. to alight from a horse, mostly with la (la), although rí-la _báb-pa (ri la 'bab pa) may also mean: to alight (flying) on a mountain Dzl. IV, 2. — 2. to fall down, gnám-la k`á-ba _bab (gnam la kha ba 'bab) snow falls from heaven Dzl. — to flow, the usual word; to flow off; mi-gtsáń _báb-pai gtór-kuń (mi gtsang 'bab pa'i gtor khung) sink-hole, for dirty water to run through Lex. — 4. to alight on, to enter into, of demons Lt. — 5. in a general sense, like to get: ńya skám-la _báb-pa (nya skam la 'bab pa) a fish that has got on dry ground; ńá-la ré-mos _bab (nga la re mos 'bab) Pth., or res _bab (res 'bab) Tar. it is my turn; sróg-la _báb-bo (srog la 'bab bo) Dzl. life is at stake; frq. in reference to time: čí-bai dús-la báb-bo ('chi ba'i dus la bab bo) it has come to the time of dying, the hour of death has arrived; without a genit.: it is time; skábs-la báb-bo (skabs la bab bo) there is now an opportunity Dzl. — _bab-čú ('bab chu) river, rivulet, brook; also rain. — _bab-stégs ('bab stegs) access or descent to the water, steps leading to a bathing-place Hind. ghâṭ. — _báb-mo* ('bab mo*) W. condescending, affable.
1. rkań-_bám (rkang 'bam) a disease of the foot Sch.: gout. — 2. _bám-yig ('bam yig) v. yi-ge (yi ge).
Cs. putrefaction, rottenness; to be putrid, rotten, cf. bám-pa (bam pa).

(vb. n. to sbár-ba (sbar ba)) 1. to burn, me _bár-bai káń-pa (me 'bar ba'i khang pa) a burning house Thgy.; to catch fire, to be ignited; to blaze Dzl.; also in reference to the passions frq.; to beam, radiate, _ód-du ('od du) in light Tar.; _bár-du rúń-ba ('bar du rung ba) Cs. combustible. — 2. to open, to begin to bloom, to blossom, frq. — 3. to talk, rattle, to be garrulous, babbling, *_bar _ó-pa me* ('bar 'o pa me) it is not worth while to talk about it C.; ko ńá-la máń-po _bar _dug (kho nga la mang po 'bar 'dug) he treats me to a long gossip C; esp. to brawl, quarrel, chide, k`a-_bár (kha 'bar) quarrelsome, brawling Mil.; máń-du bár-du byúń-ba-las (mang du 'bar du byung ba las) as she was going to brawl still longer Mil.; *_bar-kád táń-če* ('bar khad tang ce) to rail at a person W. — 4. dpal _bar-ba (dpal 'bar ba) Cs. to be celebrated, famous.
1. sbst. a high, pointed hill, cf. _bag-_bóg ('bag 'bog). — 2. adj. uneven, rough; pock-marked.

used only with skra (skra), 1. to part, dress, arrange, the hair, as it is customary with the monks and nuns of certain sects; in Kham also national costume; skra gyas _bal gyon _bal byéd-pa (skra g.yas 'bal g.yon 'bal byed pa) (of a nun) Pth.; *_bál-_go-čén* ('bal mgo chen) a person wearing the hair thus dressed C.; skrá-_bal-čan (skra 'bal can), prob. id.; C.: name of an old Indian sect. — 2. as a sign of mourning, to have the hair disheveled, hanging down in disorder Pth.; so also Dzl. XV, 17, acc. to correct reading; _bal-_bál ('bal 'bal) shaggy Sch.

pf. pigs (phigs), fut. dbig (dbig), imp. pig(s) (phig(s)) and _búg(s)-pa ('bug(s) pa), p`ug (phug), dbug (dbug), p`ug (phug), also p`íg-pa (phig pa), p`úg-pa (phug pa), 1. to sting, of insects Stg.; to pierce, rdó-rje-yis ni rín-čén p`ug (rdo rje yis ni rin chen phug) the diamond pierces the precious stone Pth.; to bore, šíń-la búg-pa (shing la 'bug pa) to bore holes into wood Glr.; in a gen. sense, to make a hole, rkáń-pa kyis p`ug (rkang pa khyis phug) the dog bit my foot Mil.; k`áń-pa _bíg-pa (khang pa 'big pa) Thgy. and elsewh., to break into, to break open; *_big gyáb-pa* ('big rgyab pa), id. C.; čú-gágs _bigs (chu 'gags 'bigs) it removes strangury Med. — 2. C. to deflower, to lie with, obscene. — *búg-če* ('bug ce) W. to make remarks on an absent person, to criticize. — bigs-byéd (bigs byed), n. p., n. of the Vindhya mountains (v. विन्ध्य).
